item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This seems somewhat excessive. The Foundation is literally depressing the local economy just to get a little more space around the house? Doesn't seem like a commensurate level of resources to fix a problem that could probably just be solved by passive surveillance and a sturdy fence. Also, I'm not sure "infrastructure" is the term you want here.

Excessive? A bit, yes. This is a combination of developing my Foundation headcanon for their protocols (this originally included a poorly veiled reference to Detroit obsolescence) and vaguely hinting at this skip being considered higher priority than is let on elsewise.

I get that this is something a lot of authors do. It also makes absolutely no sense that a clandestine agency would advertise itself in this manner.

Some people like this shtick, some people don't. I'm the former; I also don't think this precludes the Foundation having fronts that don't fulfill the acronym gimmick.

This part's redundant; you can just say "verify" here and it would be fine.

Point taken, will be edited.

For the additional comments:

+ show block
– hide block

I feel like the reader is lead along enough here so it's fair to agree to disagree. The intruders are tied to the GOI by the interview, where it's also expressed that they wish to connect with the killer-POI. The killer-POI, in his writing of the note, expresses:

  • A previously undocumented desire to communicate. Deviation in m.o. for serial killers is noteworthy.
  • Potential-bordering-on-probable understanding of being spectated, given that his murders have as of yet not been discovered elsewise. As-of-yet undetermined by Foundation whether, if this is the case, he is attempting to communicate to the GOI (more probable) or the Foundation.
  • Understanding of some fundamental belief in a similar way to the GOI.

I think this develops a significant enough potential narrative path for the GOI, the POI, and their eventual direct connection.

I do appreciate your comments on this and the other piece I have up, even if we've not quite seen eye-to-eye.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ minor issues
- hide text

Manipulation of local economic infrastructure has been implemented to reduce residential and industrial population density in the immediate area of Containment Site 2377

This seems somewhat excessive. The Foundation is literally depressing the local economy just to get a little more space around the house? Doesn't seem like a commensurate level of resources to fix a problem that could probably just be solved by passive surveillance and a sturdy fence. Also, I'm not sure "infrastructure" is the term you want here.

Procurement of SCP-2377-1 was completed on 10/14/199█ via Shinjuku Complex Properties, a Foundation front company.

I get that this is something a lot of authors do. It also makes absolutely no sense that a clandestine agency would advertise itself in this manner.

Due to an inability to independently verify the veracity of events

This part's redundant; you can just say "verify" here and it would be fine.

The initial murdervision thing in the house concept is decent enough, and I think it could serve as a good anchor to an article. I also think your decision to flesh it out a bit with a story is a good one, as this would need a little more depth to it past the premise in order to be a fully-realized piece.

Unfortunately, the story that this has been given isn't really doing the article much good. When you introduce a new element to a narrative, it's okay to have it give the reader mere hints and suggestions to a larger narrative, but in this article the four people breaking into the house followed by cryptic message thing isn't really leading me anywhere. It's just sort of (from the reader's perspective) random mysterious stuff. Since it doesn't show any possible path(s) for the narrative to go further, it comes across to me as filler text; lengthening the story without really adding to it.

After reading your spoilers, I think what you're going for in the larger story could be interesting. But nothing that you said in your collapsible really came through to me in the story, and so the reading experience becomes dependent on an external source to carry the weight that you want to give it. I would recommend weaving a little more of those proposed story elements into the narrative, especially if your intention is to use this as the basis for further works.

item.title: Re: Dilly's first SCP
item.content:encoded:
+ Headcanon, activate!
- IT WAS ALL A DREAM OK NOT REALLY

My concept on this is that the four who attempted to gain access to the house are members of the GOI, which is also responsible for the phenomenon. The phenomenon is difficult to achieve but replicable and the group as a whole will eventually move on to focusing on their next location but, at the least, would like to deactivate 2377 so that outsiders don't have access.

2377 is their church, in a manner of speaking. The act of murder, specifically in these cases of the victim's isolation, is important to them. I don't want to go over all the whats and whys yet as I intend to eventually have more skips and tales with the GOI becoming more fully understood.

A rewrite consideration I've had is having the incident log extend to several break-in attempts of increasing size, but I've not decided if I want to do that yet or to what degree. It may help relay the importance of 2377 to the GOI though.

item.title: Re: Dilly's first SCP
item.content:encoded: I like this but I'd love a bit of spoilered or collapsed explanation. It's nibbling at the edges for me, but what's your headcanon?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's good, but feels unresolved to me. I know that's just a personal preference though, this is a great SCP on objective merits. Good job! :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That was what's technically referred to as a "whoopsiedoodle". Fixed, thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The perpetrator of the homicide and the victim, designated SCP-2377-a and SCP-2377-b respectively

In the cases of confirmed deceased SCP-2377-a instances, ██ SCP-2377-b instances have been identified and suspected or arrested of the homicide.

I like the Article but this confuse me a little is this intended or a mess up?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You are correct; I had at some point intended to remove or augment that line in reference to the exception. I went back and did a strikethrough plus footnote. Thanks for the catch and the upvote!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Instances of SCP-2377-a and SCP-2377-b are unique to each scenario.

Comparative analysis of multiple witnesses' physical descriptions of SCP-2377-a instances has lead to the designation of POI-2377-02, an individual determined to be responsible for the homicide event in at least ██ SCP-2377 scenarios.

This is a contradiction.

Other than that, I find this decent enough to upvote.

item.title: Dilly's first SCP
item.content:encoded: So here we go. Thanks to those who gave it a lookover and offered comments: A Random Day, TwistedGears, Saltmine, and MadCatUSA.
